repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem

- Windows users can't use terminfo to configure their terminal capabilities #37274
- Terminfo definitions sometimes get out of date or are simply inaccurate
- Eventually, we may want to get rid of terminfo, relying primarily on built-in definitions. Users will still need some flexibility

## Solution

Allow overriding terminfo definitions using JSON in the $NVIM_TERMINFO environment variable.

close #37274

See https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37475#issuecomment-4343192380

comment_data.body: instead of doing all this manually in C, can we use `nlua_call_vimfn` or `NLUA_EXEC_STATIC` to call a Lua function defined in `runtime/lua/vim/tty.lua` which does as much as possible?

If NVIM_TERMINFO (name TBD) is defined, we should skip `terminfo_from_database` and all terminfo handling entirely.

That clearly sets expectations, and positions us to fully remove terminfo handling in the future.
makes sense to me, but cc @bfredl in case there was an explicit reason for `has_Tc_or_RGB`
not really excited about maintaining this list manually. I suppose that shouldn't block this PR, but we should plan to followup with a `gen_vimdoc.lua` change that generates this somehow.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -50,6 +50,99 @@ own terminfo is usually as simple as running this:
   gunzip terminfo.src.gz
   tic -x terminfo.src
 <
+
+                                                       *$NVIM_TERMINFO*
+To override terminfo entries without modifying terminfo files, use
+$NVIM_TERMINFO. This may be useful on Windows, where Unibilium can't read
+terminfo files. This variable should be a json object with any of the
+following keys. Be careful when putting escape sequences in strings, as json
+uses unicode escapes like `\u001b.
+>
+  Key                        Type
+  ------------------------------------------------------------
+  carriage_return            string
+  change_scroll_region       string

comment_data.body: Yeah, I'll give it a try. Very new to the Lua/C interop.

Are you saying we would skip the builtin defs too? My thinking was that this would allow users to provide a few overrides for things that are broken, not that they'd have to provide full terminfo (although that is an option). It seems like the things users have mentioned most with regard to terminfo are alternate screen settings, reset cursor style, and home/end.
Sure, I can do that as a followup.

comment_data.body: oh sorry, no, i only meant to skip the system defs.

I felt a little weird not including all the variables as arguments to the macro, but I didn't think it'd be used anywhere else, so I tried to keep it minimal. Let me know if I should add args for everything or turn these into functions instead of macros. I don't know what's "idiomatic."

EDIT: I saw `TI_HAS` and followed the same pattern of defining it inside the function where it's used.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -354,6 +359,136 @@ void tui_query_bg_color(TUIData *tui)
   flush_buf(tui);
 }
 
+typedef struct {
+  char *name;
+  int idx;
+} TerminfoCapMap;
+
+typedef struct {
+  char *name;
+  int idx;
+  bool shift;  // Only used for keys that have a shift variant
+} TerminfoKeyMap;
+
+static const TerminfoCapMap ti_defs[] = {
+#define X(n) { .name = #n, .idx = kTerm_##n },
+  XLIST_TERMINFO_BUILTIN
+#undef X
+#define X(n, code) { .name = #n, .idx = kTerm_##n },
+  XLIST_TERMINFO_EXT
+#undef X
+};
+
+static const TerminfoKeyMap ti_keys[] = {
+#define X(n) { .name = "key_" #n, .idx = kTermKey_##n, .shift = false },
+#define Y(n) { .name = "key_" #n, .idx = kTermKey_##n, .shift = true },
+  XYLIST_TERMINFO_KEYS
+#undef X
+#undef Y
+};
+
+static const char *ti_fkey_names[] = {
+#define X(n) "key_" #n,
+  XLIST_TERMINFO_FKEYS
+#undef X
+};
+
+#define LUA_FIELD_OVERRIDE_STR(field, dst) \
+  do { \
+    ADD_C(fields, CSTR_AS_OBJ(field)); \
+    ADD_C(args, ARRAY_OBJ(fields)); \
+    rv = NLUA_EXEC_STATIC("return require('vim.tty').get_terminfo_override(...)", \
+                          args, kRetObject, NULL, &lua_err); \
+    if (!ERROR_SET(&lua_err) && rv.type == kObjectTypeString) { \
+      dst = arena_strdup(&tui->ti_arena, rv.data.string.data); \
+    } \
+    fields.size--; \
+    args.size--; \
+    api_free_object(rv); \
+    api_clear_error(&lua_err); \
+  } while (0)
+
+#define LUA_FIELD_OVERRIDE_BOOL(field, dst) \
+  do { \
+    ADD_C(fields, CSTR_AS_OBJ(field)); \
+    ADD_C(args, ARRAY_OBJ(fields)); \
+    rv = NLUA_EXEC_STATIC("return require('vim.tty').get_terminfo_override(...)", \
+                          args, kRetObject, NULL, &lua_err); \
+    if (!ERROR_SET(&lua_err) && rv.type == kObjectTypeBoolean) { \
+      dst = rv.data.boolean; \
+    } \
+    fields.size--; \
+    args.size--; \
+    api_free_object(rv); \
+    api_clear_error(&lua_err); \
+  } while (0)
+
+#define LUA_FIELD_OVERRIDE_INT(field, dst) \
+  do { \
+    ADD_C(fields, CSTR_AS_OBJ(field)); \
+    ADD_C(args, ARRAY_OBJ(fields)); \
+    rv = NLUA_EXEC_STATIC("return require('vim.tty').get_terminfo_override(...)", \
+                          args, kRetObject, NULL, &lua_err); \
+    if (!ERROR_SET(&lua_err) && rv.type == kObjectTypeInteger) { \
+      dst = (int)rv.data.integer; \
+    } \
+    fields.size--; \
+    args.size--; \
+    api_free_object(rv); \
+    api_clear_error(&lua_err); \
+  } while (0)

comment_data.body: Alright, now it's getting all the JSON values from `vim.tty.get_terminfo_override`. Perhaps `NVIM_TERMINFO_OVERRIDES` would be a better name for the variable.
Done. Updated docs to point this out explicitly.
as @ofseed called out, it's probably too confusing to name this "terminfo". how about `NVIM_TERMDEFS` or `NVIM_TERMCODES` ? for reference, Vim calls its `t_xx` options `terminal-key-codes`

comment_data.body:
use `error` for errors. also, it looks like `E557` would make sense for us to re-use, from vim: https://github.com/vim/vim/blob/5c700152ae23c91b6edef3fa3e7ba06d40be0f9e/src/po/vi.po#L4405


```suggestion
    error(
      'E557: Failed to parse $NVIM_TERMDEFS: ' .. vim.inspect(overrides_or_err),
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -158,4 +158,47 @@ function M.query_apc(payload, opts, on_response)
   end)
 end
 
+local terminfo_overrides ---@type table | nil
+local terminfo_overrides_raw = os.getenv('NVIM_TERMINFO')
+if terminfo_overrides_raw ~= nil then
+  local ok, overrides_or_err = pcall(vim.json.decode, terminfo_overrides_raw)
+  if ok then
+    terminfo_overrides = overrides_or_err
+  elseif #vim.api.nvim_list_uis() > 0 then
+    -- This file is sourced during the TUI startup, but we only want to notify
+    -- when it fails on the server
+    vim.notify(
+      'error decoding $NVIM_TERMINFO: ' .. vim.inspect(overrides_or_err),
+      vim.log.levels.ERROR

comment_data.body: is this silently ignoring an error case?

@@ -158,4 +158,47 @@ function M.query_apc(payload, opts, on_response)
   end)
 end
 
+local terminfo_overrides ---@type table | nil
+local terminfo_overrides_raw = os.getenv('NVIM_TERMINFO')
+if terminfo_overrides_raw ~= nil then
+  local ok, overrides_or_err = pcall(vim.json.decode, terminfo_overrides_raw)
+  if ok then
+    terminfo_overrides = overrides_or_err
+  elseif #vim.api.nvim_list_uis() > 0 then
+    -- This file is sourced during the TUI startup, but we only want to notify
+    -- when it fails on the server
+    vim.notify(
+      'error decoding $NVIM_TERMINFO: ' .. vim.inspect(overrides_or_err),
+      vim.log.levels.ERROR
+    )
+  end
+end
+
+--- Get terminfo capabilities stored in $NVIM_TERMINFO. `fields` is
+--- an array used to access nested fields within the JSON. For example, if
+--- fields is `{'key_home', 'base'}`, it accesses
+--- `terminfo_overrides.key_home.base`. Pass an empty list to get the whole
+--- object.
+---
+--- Returns nil if the field is empty.
+---
+---@param fields string[]
+---@return any
+function M.get_terminfo_override(fields)
+  vim.validate('fields', fields, 'table')
+  if type(terminfo_overrides) ~= 'table' then
+    return
+  end
+
+  local res = terminfo_overrides
+  for _, field in ipairs(fields) do
+    if type(field) ~= 'string' or type(res) ~= 'table' then
+      return

comment_data.body: i still think we could reduce the boilerplate here by 3x. instead of calling into Lua multiple times, just call one time and return a big map.
Done. Had to put it in an autocmd, or else it wasn't displaying.

comment_data.body:
Tried to refactor it and make it as linear as possible. Now it iterates over the JSON and uses a map to lookup the offsets into the TerminfoEntry struct, so it can put values in there quite directly. There's still quite a bit of boilerplate just to set up the offset data, but I'm not sure how I would simplify that. I could generate some of the boilerplate from Lua, but that's just moving the cruft somewhere else.

EDIT: Actually, maybe I will go ahead and generate some of the boilerplate from Lua. I'll need all the data organized nicely in Lua to generate the docs anyways, and that way the bools and numbers will stay in sync.
Hopefully better now. I added macro definitions for the terminfo bools and ints, and I extracted the terminfo data to a separate Lua file to be used in the doc generation.
